narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because CSM scores USD at 20 with a -0.58 proxy reading despite nominal DXY tick higher, indicating distribution not accumulation
2. JPY selling pressure because all JPY crosses show H4 sell signals with USDJPY leading, driven by risk-on equity gains and falling US yields compressing carry logic
3. CHF and GBP strength because CSM ranks CHF at 100 and GBP at 91, with GBPUSD carrying aligned D1 and H4 buy signals as the only fully confirmed directional setup

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD is the cleanest expression of the dominant narrative with both timeframes aligned buy and GBP scoring highest among non-CHF currencies. AUDUSD and NZDUSD are confirming the broad USD softness with H4 buy signals, consistent with their 0.93 mutual correlation moving in lockstep. USDCHF sell signal reinforces CHF dominance and cross-validates EURUSD weakness is EUR-specific, not USD strength, since EURUSD lacks a buy signal despite USD being weak.

REGIME

Mixed, conflicted. VIX at 17.0 is elevated and rising slightly, S&P 500 is up 0.29 percent supporting risk-on, but gold at 4630 up on the day signals concurrent safe-haven demand, making this a split risk environment rather than clean risk-on.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. USD bias: fundamentals label USD bullish on trade tensions but CSM, DXY proxy at -0.58, and all USD pairs showing sell signals collectively reject this narrative as stale or fading
2. Risk regime: risk-on label conflicts with VIX rising 0.59 percent and gold gaining simultaneously, suggesting equity strength is not accompanied by genuine risk appetite expansion

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: GBPUSD up - only fully aligned D1 and H4 buy with strongest non-CHF CSM - Conviction 7/10
Secondary: USDCHF down - CHF at 100 CSM, H4 and D1 sell aligned, CHF dominance unambiguous - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: EURUSD - EUR is weakest currency at CSM zero, blunts USD weakness trade entirely
Watch: DXY breaking below 98.00 confirms USD distribution and activates full conviction on GBPUSD long
key_observation: Dollar rebounded on resurging trade tensions despite broad risk-on sentiment from record equity highs. Commodity strength particularly evident in agricultural and soft commodities, suggesting some dollar weakness pressure underneath. Conflicting signals present with USD strength headline contradicted by short covering in coffee on dollar weakness.
watch: Trade tension escalation developments. Equity momentum sustainability. Commodity price persistence. Risk-off trigger potential if geopolitical tensions intensify.
